analysis.consistency: High. The spell mechanics are clearly defined for both creature and object manipulation, consistently requiring an action each round for concentration spells.
analysis.quality: High. The description is detailed, outlining contested checks for creatures and carried objects, and specifying movement limits and control capabilities (fine control). The 'restrained' condition on a creature is a significant mechanical effect.

mechanics.action_economy: Requires an action every round to maintain effect on a target.

creature_control.effect: Move up to 30 feet; target becomes [restrained] until the end of the caster's next turn.
creature_control.check: Caster Spellcasting Ability Check vs. Target Strength Check.

object_manipulation.attended_move: Requires Caster Spellcasting Ability Check vs. Carrier's Strength Check to pull object away and move it up to 30 feet.
object_manipulation.fine_control: Explicitly allowed for simple tasks (tools, opening doors, pouring).

limitations: Creature grip requires repeating contest each round to maintain the restrained condition.

analysis.conformance: The data appears to conform to standard D&D 5e spell structure and terminology.
